relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Wise-hearted makers do the work YHWH commanded.
Those lifted in heart draw near to make.
Freewill gifts arrive morning by morning.
The artisans pause their work to report.
The people have brought more than enough.
Moses stops the giving so holy work does not become extraction.
There is sufficient, and more.
Wise-hearted hands weave the cherubim curtains.
Each curtain receives its measure.
Curtains are joined into sets.
Blue loops prepare the joining.
Loop faces loop.
Gold clasps make the dwelling one.
Goats’ hair curtains shelter the dwelling.
The tent curtains receive measure.
Five and six are joined into shelter.
Loops form the edges.
Bronze clasps make the tent one.
Ram skins and durable hides cover above.
Acacia frames stand upright.
Each frame is measured.
Tenons join the structure.
Twenty frames stand southward.
Silver bases hold them.
Twenty frames stand northward.
Silver bases hold the north.
Six frames form the rear.
Corner frames strengthen the back.
The corners are doubled into one.
Eight frames rest on sixteen bases.
Acacia bars bind one side.
Bars bind the other side and rear.
The middle bar passes end to end.
Gold covers frames, rings, and bars.
The veil is woven with cherubim.
Four gold-covered pillars hold the veil.
The entrance screen is embroidered in sacred colors.
Five pillars, gold tops, and bronze bases hold the entry.

contemplative_note: Exodus 36 is sacred craft without extraction: the people give more than enough, Moses stops the offering, and wise-hearted hands make the dwelling one.
